content: Acetaminophen (e.g., Tylenol) is metabolized into n-acetyl-p-benzoquinone, which is toxic to the liver and requires glutathione to neutralize; excessive intake can lead to liver cell death and fibrosis.
Alcohol consumption converts into acetaldehyde, which the liver must metabolize; excessive alcohol leads to insufficient glutathione production, resulting in cell death and fibrosis.
Smoking increases free radicals in the liver, leading to fibrosis and impaired liver function; the liver's regeneration occurs during sleep, and lack of sleep impairs this process.
Curcumin, found in turmeric, promotes liver cell apoptosis and increases glutathione levels, helping to protect against liver damage and oxidative stress.
